title: Skills You Now Have
concept: You can now explain autoencoders, VAEs, GANs, and diffusion models, and reason about when and how to apply each for a given generative task.

exercises.prompt: Now that you know multiple generative models, what is the best way to start a new AI project?

options.text: Analyze the specific needs (speed, quality, stability) of my task
Always use Diffusion models for every single task
Always use basic Autoencoders because they are the oldest

exercises.explain: Choosing the right model depends entirely on your project's requirements, such as speed, image sharpness, or training stability.

cards.title: Master of Creation
cards.body: You've journeyed through the worlds of Autoencoders, VAEs, GANs, and Diffusion models. Now, it's time to step back and look at your new superpowers! You can now choose the exact right AI model for any creative or technical task.

cards.title: Your Generative Toolkit
cards.body: Generative AI models are tools with different strengths. Autoencoders compress data, VAEs smoothly generate variations by mapping data to a bell curve, GANs use a generator-discriminator duel to make sharp images, and Diffusion models generate high-quality details by removing noise step-by-step.

cards.title: The Generative Garage
cards.body: Think of your generative AI toolkit like a garage full of vehicles. An Autoencoder is like a trash compactor that squashes and cleans junk. A VAE is a magical cloning machine that makes smooth variations. A GAN is a high-speed racing duel between two drivers. A Diffusion model is a sculptor slowly carving a masterpiece out of a block of marble.

cards.title: Matching Tool to Task
cards.body: If you want to build a face-swapping app, a GAN is perfect because it creates sharp, realistic faces quickly. If you want to compress medical scans to save space, a standard Autoencoder is ideal. If you want to morph one face smoothly into another, a VAE is your best bet.

cards.title: The Perfect Model Myth
cards.body: Many beginners think there is one 'best' AI model that beats all others. In reality, there is always a trade-off! GANs are fast but tricky to train, Diffusion models make the best images but are slow, and VAEs are stable but can produce blurry results. Choose based on your project's needs!

cards.title: GANs vs. Diffusion
cards.body: Let's compare the two giants of modern image generation: GANs and Diffusion models.

left.text: Super fast generation in a single pass, but can be unstable to train.

right.text: Slow generation due to many denoising steps, but highly stable and detailed.

exercises.prompt: Which model would you choose if you want to smoothly transition (interpolate) between two different shoe designs?

options.text: A basic Autoencoder, because it only compresses data
A Variational Autoencoder (VAE), because of its smooth latent space
A GAN, because it is easy and stable to train
None of these models can handle image tasks

exercises.explain: VAEs map input data to a continuous probability distribution (a smooth latent space), which makes them excellent for smoothly morphing or interpolating between different designs.

exercises.prompt: A standard Autoencoder is the best model for generating high-quality, completely new random images from scratch.
exercises.explain: Standard autoencoders compress and reconstruct data, but they lack a continuous latent space, making them poor choices for generating brand-new, random images.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank with the model type that excels at generating the most detailed, high-quality images.
exercises.sentence: If your main goal is ultimate image quality and detail, a _ model is the best choice.
exercises.explain: Diffusion models are currently the top choice for generating highly detailed, photorealistic images, even though they require multiple steps to remove noise.

exercises.prompt: An AI model is set up as a competition between two networks: one creating fake currency and the other trying to spot it. What is this?
exercises.explain: The generator creates fake images to fool the discriminator, while the discriminator tries to detect the fakes. This adversarial game is unique to GANs.

exercises.prompt: You are hired to build a state-of-the-art AI art generator that creates stunning, photorealistic fantasy landscapes. Which model is your best primary choice and why?

options.text: A standard Autoencoder to compress the canvas size
A Variational Autoencoder (VAE) to ensure fast, blurry drafts
A GAN, because it never suffers from training instability
A Diffusion model to slowly paint detail out of pure noise

exercises.explain: For high-fidelity, creative artwork, Diffusion models (like Midjourney or Stable Diffusion) are the gold standard because they generate extremely detailed images by refining noise. GANs are fast but hard to train, VAEs can be blurry, and standard Autoencoders don't generate new creative images easily.
